mission.title: Install and Configure kubeadm (Kubernetes bootstrapper)
mission.description: Kubeadm is a tool designed to simplify the process of creating and managing Kubernetes clusters. It provides a straightforward way to bootstrap a Kubernetes control plane and join worker nodes. While it offers a quick setup, users must manage the underlying infrastructure and networking configurations themselves.

steps.title: Install Kubeadm
steps.description:
This step installs the kubeadm binary on your system, which is necessary for bootstrapping the Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

curl -s https://packages.cloud.google.com/apt/doc/apt-key.gpg | sudo apt-key add -

echo 'deb https://apt.kubernetes.io/ kubernetes-xenial main' | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/kubernetes.list

sudo apt-get update

sudo apt-get install -y kubelet=1.31.0-00 kubeadm=1.31.0-00 kubectl=1.31.0-00

sudo apt-mark hold kubelet kubeadm kubectl

```

> Add Google Cloud's GPG key.
> Add the Kubernetes APT repository.
> Update the package list.
> Install specific versions of kubelet, kubeadm, and kubectl.
> Prevent automatic updates of these packages.
commands: echo 'deb https://apt.kubernetes.io/ kubernetes-xenial main' | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/kubernetes.list
sudo apt-get install -y kubelet=1.31.0-00 kubeadm=1.31.0-00 kubectl=1.31.0-00
sudo apt-mark hold kubelet kubeadm kubectl

steps.title: Initialize the Kubernetes Control Plane
steps.description:
This command initializes the Kubernetes control plane on the master node.

```bash

sudo kubeadm init --kubernetes-version v1.31.0 --pod-network-cidr=192.168.0.0/16

```

> Initialize the cluster with a specific Kubernetes version and pod network CIDR.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig for the Root User
steps.description:
This step configures kubeconfig for the root user to interact with the Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

mkdir -p $HOME/.kube

sudo cp -i /etc/kubernetes/admin.conf $HOME/.kube/config

sudo chown $(id -u):$(id -g) $HOME/.kube/config

```

> Create the kube directory.
> Copy the admin config file.
> Change ownership of the config file.

steps.title: Install a Pod Network Add-on
steps.description:
This step installs a network add-on to allow communication between pods.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://docs.projectcalico.org/manifests/calico.yaml

```

> Install Calico as the networking solution.

steps.title: Verify the Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in the cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes and their statuses.

uninstall.title: Reset the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command will remove all Kubernetes components and configurations from the node. Data loss may occur if persistent volumes are not managed properly.

```bash

sudo kubeadm reset

sudo rm -rf /etc/kubernetes

sudo rm -rf $HOME/.kube

```

> Reset the kubeadm configuration.
> Remove Kubernetes configuration files.
> Remove kubeconfig files.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your cluster state. This step will upgrade the Kubernetes version and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get nodes -o wide

sudo kubeadm upgrade plan

sudo kubeadm upgrade apply v1.31.0

kubectl get nodes

```

> Check the current node versions.
> Check available upgrades.
> Upgrade the control plane.
> Verify that nodes are updated.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubelet not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubelet service may not be started or is failing to run.

**Fix:**
Run 'sudo systemctl status kubelet' to check the status and 'sudo systemctl start kubelet' to start it.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubeadm init failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to insufficient resources or misconfigured network settings.

**Fix:**
Ensure your node meets the minimum resource requirements and check the network settings.

**Affected versions:** <= 1.30

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'node not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the node is not ready to accept pods, often due to network issues.

**Fix:**
Check the network add-on installation and ensure it is configured correctly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubelet configuration file not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubelet configuration file may be missing or incorrectly specified.

**Fix:**
Ensure the kubelet configuration file exists at /var/lib/kubelet/config.yaml.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for the new PodSecurity admission controller, improved kubelet configuration options.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the use of --pod-network-cidr flag in favor of network plugins.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.30, ensure to review the new PodSecurity policies and adjust configurations accordingly.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get nodes' showing your control plane and worker nodes in 'Ready' status.

sudo apt-get install -y kubelet=1.31.0-00 kubeadm=1.31.0-00 kubectl=1.31.0-00
sudo apt-mark hold kubelet kubeadm kubectl

prerequisites.description: Ensure your system meets the minimum resource requirements and has the necessary tools installed.
